methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $44M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Housing & Construction (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Housing & Construction, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Cobod
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Material Evolution
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Inversion Space is a Series A-stage company in Housing & Construction founded by Justin Fiaschetti and Austin Briggs. The target has raised $44M to date. The Housing & Construction sector contains a limited comp set: three private peers (ICON, Cobod, Material Evolution) and no public comparables. The narrow peer universe and stage-specificity constraints limit multi-method triangulation.

Stage-Progression methodology is the primary applicable valuation approach. Using total capital raised ($44M) as a proxy for Series A valuation and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to later-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $88M. Peer-Median analysis was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparable data points within the Housing & Construction sector; a forced sector-wide median would lack specificity and introduce unwarranted noise.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $75M to $101M, with a median of $88M. This range reflects the primary reliance on stage-progression mechanics anchored to $44M raised. Key uncertainties center on the early-stage operational specifics of Inversion Space, the representativeness of the 2.0x step-up multiplier for this particular sector and cohort, and the thinness of the Housing & Construction comp set. Additional data on peer-stage financials and Inversion Space's unit economics would refine conviction in this range.
